name: Sierra de Hornachuelos - Las Navas de la Concepción Loop
name_original: Las Navas de la Concepción desde Hornachuelos — circular por el Parque Natural Sierra de Hornachuelos
description: This difficult 45.4-mile road cycling route through Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park offers stunning views and a significant physical cha
region_intro: Road cycling around Hornachuelos offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park, part of the Sierra Morena mountain range. The region features a landscape of gentle hills, river valleys, and elevated plains, providing a dynamic backdrop for cycling. Routes often traverse Mediterranean woodlands of holm oaks and cork oaks, alongside picturesque reservoirs formed by rivers like the Bembézar. This area presents a rolling terrain with numerous hills, suitable for various fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Hornachuelos The most popular road cycling route is Sierra de Hornachuelos - Las Navas de la Concepción Loop, a 45.4 miles (73.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 29 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the natural park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Parque Natural Sierra de Hornachuelos — circular from Hornachuelos, a difficult 100.2 miles (161.3 km) path. This route explores the varied topography and woodlands of the natural park. Local road cyclists also love the Hornachuelos Dam – Bembézar Dam loop from Paterna, a 40.4 miles (65.0 km) trail leading through river valleys and past reservoirs, often completed in about 2 hours 45 minutes. Road cycling around Hornachuelos is defined by Mediterranean woodlands, river valleys, and rolling terrain. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Hornachuelos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 850 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Hornachuelos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Hornachuelos?
A: There are over 60 road cycling routes available around Hornachuelos, catering to various fitness levels. These routes explore the diverse landscapes of the Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for road cycling routes in Hornachuelos?
A: Hornachuelos offers a range of difficulty levels. You'll find approximately 6 easy routes, 38 moderate routes, and 23 difficult routes. The region is known for its 'relentless rolling terrain' with numerous hills, providing a continuous challenge for those seeking it.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in Hornachuelos?
A: The best seasons for road cycling in Hornachuelos are typically spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler days. The region's Mediterranean climate makes it suitable for cycling for much of the year.

Q: Are there many circular road cycling routes in Hornachuelos?
A: Yes, Hornachuelos features several scenic circular road cycling routes. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010629297/sierra-de-hornachuelos-las-navas-de-la-concepcion-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Hornachuelos - Las Navas de la Concepción Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016889495/parque-natural-sierra-de-hornachuelos-circular-from-hornachuelos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural Sierra de Hornachuelos — circular from Hornachuelos&lt;/a&gt;, which allow you to start and end in the same location while exploring the natural park.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while road cycling in Hornachuelos?
A: Road cycling in Hornachuelos takes you through breathtaking Mediterranean woodlands dominated by holm oaks, cork oaks, and gall oaks. You'll also traverse river valleys and pass picturesque reservoirs like the Bembézar reservoir, offering stunning panoramic views, especially from elevated viewpoints.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Hornachuelos?
A: While many routes feature rolling terrain and significant elevation, there are easier options available. Look for routes categorized as 'easy' in the komoot guide, which are generally shorter and have less elevation gain, making them more suitable for families or beginners.

Q: What are some notable attractions or viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Along the routes, you can enjoy views of natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5617701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bembézar Dam&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/919273" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornachuelos Dam&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5340608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Seminario de los Ángeles&lt;/a&gt; offers particularly stunning vistas over the Bembézar River.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Hornachuelos?
A: The road cycling routes in Hornachuelos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Cyclists often praise the quiet, well-paved roads, the spectacular views of the natural park, and the challenging yet rewarding rolling terrain.

Q: Can I spot any wildlife while cycling in the Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park?
A: Yes, the Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park is a sanctuary for diverse wildlife. While cycling, you might spot various bird species, including griffon vultures, and potentially other animals like red deer or wild boar, especially in quieter sections of the park.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs or dams?
A: Absolutely. Several routes lead through river valleys and past significant reservoirs. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24523297" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornachuelos Dam – Bembézar Dam loop from Paterna&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights these impressive water features, offering scenic views along the way.

Q: What kind of terrain defines road cycling in Hornachuelos?
A: Road cycling in Hornachuelos is defined by its 'relentless rolling terrain.' You'll encounter numerous hills and passes, providing a dynamic and engaging ride without extended, arduous climbs. The routes often feature well-paved surfaces through woodlands and mountains.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists seeking a significant challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016889495/parque-natural-sierra-de-hornachuelos-circular-from-hornachuelos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural Sierra de Hornachuelos — circular from Hornachuelos&lt;/a&gt; offer substantial distances (over 160 km) and considerable elevation gain, exploring the varied topography of the natural park.
tips:
San Calixto is a convent of the Discalced Carmelite Order of Tardón, it is located 17 kilometers from Hornachuelos (Córdoba), in the Sierra de Hornachuelos area.
It is a very nice cycling destination with a stretched 15 km ascent, with some area where you can recover your legs. If we follow the road we will go further into the Sierra de Hornachuelos entering the province of Seville and more specifically in the town of Las Navas de la Concepción.

If you stop to rest in Las Navas, take a walk through the Plaza de España. There you will find a bar where you can recharge your batteries, besides there is a beautiful Fountain of the Lions and a fun monument by Manuel Carballido. Also look for the tile with olive trees, dedicated to the working rural woman.
Built in 1970, its large hydrographic basin is dominated by the typical Mediterranean forest of holm oaks and cork oaks. Here you can see cormorants, herons and various species of ducks.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a difficult or hard bike ride, requiring very good fitness. It features relentless rolling terrain with numerous hills and passes, making it very demanding despite not having exceptionally long climbs.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is mostly paved, offering a paradise for smoother, more comfortable riding. However, be aware that some segments may be unpaved and difficult to ride, which is an important consideration for road cyclists.

faqs.question: Is this a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular route, starting and ending in Hornachuelos, taking you through the Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park and past Las Navas de la Concepción.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: While specific parking spots aren't detailed, Hornachuelos serves as the starting point. You should look for suitable parking within or on the outskirts of Hornachuelos before beginning your ride.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park is beautiful year-round, but for cycling, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring cooler, wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers spectacular views and passes several points of interest. You'll go near the Convent of San Calixto, a small monastery nestled in the sierra. You'll also pass through the settlement of Las Navas de la Concepción, where the Plaza de España offers a place to rest. The route also skirts the Retortillo Reservoir.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with other paths. You will find sections that overlap with the Sendero de Sierra Morena, and smaller portions of the CHG - Embalse del Retortillo and Avenida de la Reina de los Ángeles trails.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its classification as a 'difficult' route requiring 'very good fitness' with significant length and elevation gain, this trail is not recommended for beginners or families with young children. It's best suited for experienced road cyclists.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: While the route is primarily for road cycling on public roads and through natural park areas, specific regulations for dogs on bikes are not typically enforced on such routes. However, always ensure your dog is well-behaved, on a leash if off the bike, and that you clean up after them, especially when passing through settlements or protected areas.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee to cycle in the Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no entrance fees or special permits required for cycling on public roads within the Sierra de Hornachuelos Natural Park. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of the natural environment.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or water along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is characterized by its 'self-sufficiency' and 'solitary roads,' meaning amenities are limited. Las Navas de la Concepción offers the Plaza de España with a bar where you can rest and refuel. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of your ride, as stops may be infrequent.
